title: Toroloom — WebSocket Connections
description: Real-time WebSocket connection monitoring for the Toroloom trading platform. Tracks active connections, per-user distribution, subscription health, tick throughput, Node.js runtime metrics, broker dispatch latency, and per-symbol subscription distribution.

panels.title: Total Connections
panels.description: Total active authenticated WebSocket connections across all users.

panels.title: Authenticated Users
panels.description: Number of distinct authenticated users with at least one active WebSocket connection.

panels.title: Alerted Users
panels.description: Users who have exceeded the max concurrent connection threshold (default: 5).

panels.title: Ticks / sec
panels.description: Running rate of price ticks delivered to clients over the last 5 minutes.

panels.title: Connections & Users Over Time
panels.description: Total active connections vs distinct authenticated users. A widening gap indicates multi-tab usage.

panels.title: Ticks Per Second
panels.description: Aggregate tick delivery rate across all users. Drops to zero indicate a ticker stall.

panels.title: Node.js Runtime
panels.description: Node.js process health metrics — event loop lag, memory usage, handles. These help detect CPU starvation, memory leaks, and socket leaks.

panels.title: Event Loop Lag
panels.description: Node.js event loop lag in milliseconds. Values above 100ms indicate blocking operations or CPU starvation.

panels.title: Heap Used
panels.description: V8 heap memory currently in use by the Node.js process. A gradual upward trend with no GC drop may indicate a memory leak.

panels.title: RSS
panels.description: Resident Set Size — total physical memory allocated to the Node.js process. High values relative to heap indicate native bindings or external allocations.

panels.title: Active Handles
panels.description: Number of active libuv handles (timers, sockets, files). A smooth upward trend may indicate a handle leak (e.g. unclosed WebSocket or timer).

panels.title: Event Loop Lag Over Time
panels.description: Event loop lag in milliseconds. Spikes above 100ms indicate blocking event-loop operations. Sustained values above 500ms suggest severe CPU starvation or synchronous I/O in the hot path.

panels.title: Memory Usage
panels.description: V8 heap (used vs total), RSS, and external memory. A used/total gap approaching zero indicates GC pressure. RSS exceeding total by a large margin points to non-heap allocations (buffers, native addons).

panels.title: Broker & Symbols
panels.description: Broker tick dispatch latency and per-symbol subscription distribution. High latency spikes indicate serialization overhead; symbols with the most subscribers are the most actively traded.

panels.title: Tick Dispatch Latency (p99)
panels.description: 99th percentile of broker tick dispatch latency over the last 5 minutes. Values above 1ms indicate serialization or backpressure bottlenecks.

panels.title: Tick Dispatch Rate
panels.description: Aggregate tick dispatch rate across all users. The same as 'Ticks / sec' in Overview but here for side-by-side comparison with latency.

panels.title: Unique Symbols
panels.description: Number of unique symbols currently subscribed to by at least one client.

panels.title: Avg Subs / Symbol
panels.description: Average number of subscribers per symbol. High values indicate concentrated interest; low values indicate broad, diversified tracking.

panels.title: Tick Dispatch Latency (p50 / p95 / p99)
panels.description: Multidimensional tick dispatch latency. Use the p50-p95 gap to detect outlier ticks causing latency spikes. If p99 is 10x p95, individual large ticks (e.g. serializing many symbols) are the bottleneck.

panels.title: Top Subscribed Symbols
panels.description: Symbols with the most active WebSocket subscribers. Sorted by subscriber count descending. Helps identify the most watched tickers and potential fan-out load per symbol.

panels.title: Infrastructure
panels.description: Broker connection health, authentication status, failover events, and WebSocket rate limiter activity.

panels.title: Broker Connected
panels.description: Current broker connection status. 1 = connected, 0 = disconnected. A drop to 0 indicates an outage or authentication failure.

panels.title: Auth Errors / min
panels.description: Rate of broker authentication failures. Spikes indicate token expiry, invalid credentials, or network issues reaching broker APIs.

panels.title: Reconnects / min
panels.description: Rate of broker reconnection or failover events. Values > 0 indicate network instability or broker API downtime.

panels.title: Rate Limited / min
panels.description: Rate of WebSocket messages rejected by the per-connection rate limiter. Sustained activity above 0 indicates aggressive clients or potential DoS.

panels.title: Broker Connection State
panels.description: Broker connection status over time. A drop to 0 triggers a failover to the next available broker in the chain (Zerodha → Angel → Mock).

panels.title: Rate Limiter Activity
panels.description: Rate-limited messages and active rate limiter connections. Spikes in red indicate aggressive clients or potential DoS. Active limiters show how many connections are currently constrained.

panels.title: Connection Count by User
panels.description: Current WebSocket connections per user. Sorted by connection count descending. Values exceeding 5 trigger alerts.

panels.title: Active Subscriptions by User
panels.description: Number of symbols each user is currently subscribed to (aggregated across all their tabs).

panels.title: Per-User Connection Distribution
panels.description: Breakdown of the top 10 users by connection count. Spikes above the threshold line indicate multi-tab abuse.

panels.title: Alerted Users Gauge
panels.description: Current number of users in alert state. An alert fires when a user exceeds 5 concurrent connections.

panels.title: Connection Health Status
panels.description: Overall health score based on the ratio of users to connections. A high ratio means heavy multi-tab usage.

panels.title: Raw Metrics Sampler
panels.description: Raw Prometheus values for debugging. Shows the last scraped sample for each toroloom_ws metric.
